name: matchviz
documentation:
# matchviz

Tools for visualizing interest points and correspondences between interest points generated alignment software for 
debugging alignment of large images.

# Functionality

At present, this project supports loading interest point information from output generated by bigstitcher and saving points or lines as neuroglancer precomputed annotations, which can be rendered in neuroglancer.

# Installation

Get the latest version from pypi:

`pip install matchviz`

## License

`matchviz` is distributed under the terms of the [MIT](https://spdx.org/licenses/MIT.html) license.

functions.name: matchviz.core.get_url
documentation: Get a URL from a zarr array or group pointing to its location in storage

functions.name: matchviz.core.translate_points_xyz
documentation: Apply a translation in world coordinates

functions.name: matchviz.core.get_percentiles
documentation: Get the 5th and 95th percentiles from of the smallest array in a multiscale group

functions.name: matchviz.core.tile_coordinate_to_rgba
documentation:
generate an RGBA value from a tile coordinate. This ensures that adjacent tiles have different
colors. It's not a nice lookup table by any measure.

functions.name: matchviz.core.tokenize_tile_coords
documentation:
Convert positions in world coordinates to positions in a space where the only coordinates
allowed are tuples of integers.

functions.name: matchviz.core.parse_url
documentation: Parse input as a URL

modules.name: matchviz
documentation: SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2024-present Davis Vann Bennett <davis.v.bennett@gmail.com>
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

functions.name: matchviz.plot_points
documentation: we assume here that there's no need to parametrize t

functions.name: matchviz.annotation.write_line_annotations
documentation:
Save a collection of paired points (i.e., lines) as neuroglancer precomputed annotations.

Lines will be stored with a `point_color` attribute, which defaults to (255, 255, 255, 255),
and a `line_color` attribute, which defaults to (255, 255, 255, 255)

functions.name: matchviz.annotation.write_point_annotations
documentation:
Save a collection of points as neuroglancer precomputed annotations.

Points will be stored with the following attributes:
- `id`, which is the id of each point,
- `size` attribute, which defaults to 10,
- `point_color`, which defaults to (127, 255, 127, 255)

classes.name: matchviz.annotation.AnnotationWriterFSSpec
documentation: An AnnotationWriter that uses FSSpec for writing, enabling cloud storage.

modules.name: matchviz.__about__
documentation: SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2024-present Davis Vann Bennett <davis.v.bennett@gmail.com>
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

functions.name: matchviz.bigstitcher.parse_idmap
documentation: convert {'0,1,beads': 0} to {('0', '1', "beads"): '0'}

functions.name: matchviz.bigstitcher.parse_matches
documentation:
Convert a name, match data, and an id mapping to a polars dataframe that contains
pairwise image matching information.

functions.name: matchviz.bigstitcher.read_interest_points
documentation:
Load interest points and optionally correspondences from a bigstitcher-formatted n5 group as
polars dataframes for a single tile.

functions.name: matchviz.bigstitcher.read_all_interest_points
documentation:
Load all the match data from the n5 datasets containing it.
Takes a URL to an n5 group emitted by bigstitcher for storing interest points, e.g.
s3://bucket/dataset/interestpoints.n5/.

This function uses a thread pool to speed things up.

functions.name: matchviz.bigstitcher.get_transforms
documentation:
Get transform of view setups referenced in bigstitcher xml data. Note that in bigstitcher metadata,
the transforms are ordered from last-applied to first-applied, but this function returns them in the reverse order,
i.e. the first transform is the first one that will be applied in the transform sequence.

functions.name: matchviz.bigstitcher.compose_transforms
documentation: Compose transforms from bigstitcher.

functions.name: matchviz.bigstitcher.save_annotations
documentation:
Load points and correspondences (matches) between a single tile an all other tiles, and save as neuroglancer
precomputed annotations.

    e.g. dataset = 'exaSPIM_3163606_2023-11-17_12-54-51'
    alignment_id = 'alignment_2024-01-09_05-00-44'

N5 is organized according to the structure defined here: https://github.com/PreibischLab/multiview-reconstruction/blob/a566bf4d6d35a7ab00d976a8bf46f1615b34b2d0/src/main/java/net/preibisch/mvrecon/fiji/spimdata/interestpoints/InterestPointsN5.java#L54

If matches are not found, then just the interest points will be saved.

functions.name: matchviz.bigstitcher.save_interest_points
documentation:
Save interest points for all tiles as collection of neuroglancer precomputed annotations. One
collection of annotations will be generated per image described in the bigstitcher metadata under
the directory name <out_prefix>/<image_name>.precomputed

classes.name: matchviz.bigstitcher.IdMap
documentation: TODO: use this instead of the raw tuple

classes.name: matchviz.bigstitcher.InterestPointsMembers
documentation:
id is a num_points X 1 array of integer IDs
loc is a num_points X ndim array of locations in work coordinates

functions.name: matchviz.cli.save_interest_points_cli
documentation: Save bigstitcher interest points from n5 to neuroglancer precomputed annotations.

functions.name: matchviz.cli.save_neuroglancer_json_cli
documentation: Generate a neuroglancer viewer state as a JSON document.

functions.name: matchviz.cli.tabulate_matches_cli
documentation: Generate a tabular representation of the correspondence metadata generated by bigstitcher.
